channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN announced a restructuring Thursday that includes some 200 layoffs, an accelerated pivot to digital operations and new TV roles for personalities like Wolf Blitzer, Jake Tapper and Audie Cornish.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: California Legislature approves $2.5B for state response to Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California will spend $2.5 billion to help the Los Angeles area recover from recent deadly wildfires under a relief package approved Thursday by state lawmakers. Both Democrats and Republicans supported the bills, and Democratic Gov.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Italy's top court upholds Amanda Knox's conviction for falsely accusing innocent man of murder
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s highest court on Thursday confirmed a slander conviction against U.S. defendant Amanda Knox for accusing an innocent man of murdering her British flatmate 17 years ago in a sensational case that polarized trial watchers on both sides of the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, KREX 5

item.title: Post Malone teams up with Oreo on limited-edition cookie flavor
item.description: Oreo has partnered with Post Malone for its newest cookie, featuring a first-of-its-kind filling for the brand.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Aerial attack helps firefighters maintain the upper hand on a huge fire north of Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Overnight water drops helped stop the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles and firefighters battled to increase containment as dangerous winds whipped up again Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Authorities are examining “very concerning online writings and social media posts” connected to the shooter who killed a female student and wounded another student in a Nashville high school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump tells Davos elite to invest in US or face tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump used an address Thursday to the World Economic Forum to promise global elites lower taxes if they bring manufacturing to the U.S. and threatened to impose tariffs if they don’t.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK and JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court allows small business registration rule to take effect, aimed at money laundering
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday revived a requirement that owners of millions of small businesses register with an arm of the Treasury Department charged with fighting money laundering and other financial crimes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wall of ice the size of Rhode Island heading toward penguin-packed island off Antarctica
item.description: The world's biggest iceberg — a wall of ice the size of Rhode Island — is lumbering toward a remote island off Antarctica that's home to millions of penguins and seals.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: College enrollment now higher than pre-pandemic levels
item.description: Some of the biggest increases were in undergraduate certificate programs, with enrollments 28.5 percent above 2019 levels.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CNN to lay off dozens as part of major restructuring
item.description: The layoffs are expected to impact about six percent of CNN's global workforce, amounting to around 200 jobs, according to a source.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Southern cities get creative to clear leftover snow and ice after rare winter storm
item.description: Southern cities largely unfamiliar with snow and ice are getting creative in their efforts to clean up from this week's winter storm.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook And Russ Bynum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greensboro neighborhood rescues pregnant dog in winter weather
item.description: “She just kept going door to door, up driveways, up on porches, and somebody noticed her and called the shelter,” John Reiland said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Fambro, NewsNation

item.title: One dog dies, another rescued in Texas animal cruelty case
item.description: "There were two dogs outside of Bartlett on a property in a chain link fence with no support. No housing out in the cold with the wind blowing, whipping up the ice and the snow into their pen.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens in Border Patrol custody after boat runs out of fuel near San Diego
item.description: A Coast Guard crew deployed a boarding team and was able to safely take 26 people representing multiple nationalities to shore
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Alabama man charged with 11 homicides -- including two mass shootings -- pleads not guilty
item.description: An Alabama man charged with homicide in two separate mass shootings pleaded not guilty in state court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: With Trump pivot back to pro-oil and gas policies, one renewable energy finds favor
item.description: As promised, President Donald Trump began reversing the country's energy policies his first day in office with a spate of orders largely favoring oil, gas and coal. But there is one renewable energy that did find favor: geothermal.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump lists 'number of reasons' for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: President Donald Trump listed a “number of reasons” for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters during an interview aired Wednesday night with Fox News’s Sean Hannity.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Nashville police investigate high school shooting suspect's 'very concerning' internet and social media posts
item.description: Details are emerging surrounding Antioch High School shooter Solomon Henderson's alleged online writings as investigators in Tennessee are looking for a motive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 San Antonio police officers shot in confrontation with suspect with 3 prior arrests
item.description: Seven San Antonio police officers were wounded after a shootout with man who was reported to be suicidal late Wednesday night. The suspect has not been identified.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a new contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? This is probably why
item.description: The official POTUS and White House accounts are managed by the White House and “change when the occupant of the White House changes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger to appear in court as defense challenges prosecution methods
item.description: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger will return to court for the first time in more than two months to argue for excluding nearly all evidence collected by police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: Authorities are examining “very concerning online writings and social media posts” connected to the shooter who killed a female student and wounded another student in a Nashville high school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: Kristin M. Hall, Travis Loller And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Over 50,000 under evacuation orders or warnings as wildfire imperils homes north of Los Angeles
item.description: CASTAIC, Calif. (AP) — More than 50,000 people were under evacuation orders or warnings Wednesday as a huge and fast-moving wildfire swept through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but fire officials said a rapid ground and air assault was giving them the upper hand.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and MARCIO JOSE SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Details have been released on how 33 children were treated in the Indiana home of reported abusers Sonja and Brian Stafford for approximately 13 years.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Sonja Stafford, 60, is charged with 62 criminal counts including neglect of a dependent, human labor trafficking, strangulation and more.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, NewsNation
